title: Iron deficiency GI review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician review of iron indices, anaemia, GI symptoms, bleeding, diet, medicines, blood donation, surgery, family history, and comorbidity.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult IDA section supports symptom, bleeding, bowel, dietary, medicine, menstrual, family, prior test, and comorbidity context plus clinician assessment without assigning cause.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines for the management of iron deficiency anaemia in adults
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international gastroenterology evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults with confirmed or suspected iron deficiency anaemia; pregnancy and paediatric populations require separate evidence.
source.setting: Primary, gastroenterology, haematology, and investigation-result review.
source.exclusions: Supports symptom, bleeding, diet, medicine, donation, surgery, family, laboratory, coeliac, upper/lower GI investigation, and follow-up documentation without diagnosing cause or generating treatment.

exact_location.heading: Initial assessment and risk context
exact_location.locator: recommendations and sections on history and initial assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician review of iron indices, anaemia, GI symptoms, bleeding, diet, medicines, blood donation, surgery, family history, and comorbidity.

assessment.text: Supports recording completed coeliac testing, endoscopy, imaging, response, recurrence, and clinician-decided follow-up without ordering tests.
assessment.rationale: The exact GI investigation and follow-up section supports recording already available laboratory, coeliac, endoscopy, or imaging results and clinician-entered follow-up without ordering tests.
